note: Talk-screenshot provenance task. The executor gets a video frame (sources/screenshot.jpg) with no channel watermark/caption/title. They must visually identify the speaker + talk and trace it to the canonical original video. The answer must be the OFFICIAL upload, not a re-upload/clip.

frame_origin.source: YouTube video iG9CE55wbtY (TED official channel) — frame, no overlay text
frame_origin.what_it_shows: a man with glasses in a dark suit jacket and open-collar shirt speaking on a dark stage with colored stage lighting, mid-gesture

canonical_answer.speaker: Sir Ken Robinson
canonical_answer.also_acceptable_canonical: the official TED.com talk page for 'Sir Ken Robinson: Do schools kill creativity?' (ted.com/talks/...)

scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: canonical = the official TED upload (YouTube iG9CE55wbtY) or the official TED.com talk page. Re-uploads, clip channels, and summaries are secondary.
scoring_anchors.anti_secondary_source: stopping at a re-upload or a blog summary without reaching the official TED source caps the score
scoring_anchors.tolerance: Accept speaker=Ken Robinson + talk='Do schools kill creativity?' as the core. Full credit requires reaching the official TED video (iG9CE55wbtY) OR the TED.com talk page. Partial credit if correctly identified but only a non-official re-upload is reached. (Note: this is the most-viewed TED talk, so identification should be feasible from the frame + reverse image search.)
